evals.prompt: List all the projects in our Azure DevOps organization
evals.expected_output: A readable list of ADO projects including 'Technology', produced by running az devops project list
expectations: Runs az devops project list (with --output table or --output json)
The output includes 'Technology' as a project name
Uses the PowerShell tool (not Bash) to run az commands on Windows

evals.prompt: Show me all active user stories in the Technology project that haven't been closed yet
evals.expected_output: A list of User Story work items in active/non-closed states, fetched via a WIQL query using az boards query
expectations: Uses az boards query with a WIQL statement
The WIQL filters by WorkItemType = 'User Story'
The WIQL filters out closed/removed items (e.g. State NOT IN ('Closed', 'Removed'))
Results include work item ID and title at minimum
Uses the PowerShell tool to run the az command

evals.prompt: Can you pull up the details for work item 4008 and show me what child items are under it?
evals.expected_output: Details of work item 4008 ('ADO Initiatives') plus a listing of its two children (IDs 3934 and 4009)
expectations: Runs az boards work-item show --id 4008 to fetch work item details
Shows the title 'ADO Initiatives' for work item 4008
Uses az boards work-item relation show --id 4008 or az boards work-item show --id 4008 --expand all to get relations
Identifies and displays child work item IDs 3934 and 4009
Fetches at least basic details (title, state, type) for each child item

evals.prompt: What git repos do we have in the Technology project?
evals.expected_output: A list of Git repositories in the Technology ADO project, produced by az repos list
expectations: Runs az repos list (with --output table or --output json)
Output includes at least one repository name
Uses the PowerShell tool to run the az command
